summarization.a: A line chart showing the change in ΔM<sub>TMA</sub> with increasing TMA exposure time at 300°C.
summarization.b: A multiple line chart showing the change ΔMTMA, and ΔMHF as a function of the exposure time.
summarization.c: A line chart showing the change in ΔM<sub>HF</sub> with increasing HF exposure time at 300°C.
summarization.d: A line chart showing the change in MCPC as a function of the temperature, between 250 and 325  °C.

d.question: Which temperature leas to the highest MCPC?
How does the temperature affect the mass change per cycle?
d.answer: As the temperature increases, the amount of mass that changes per cycle decreases, slowing the ALE cycle down.

b.question: How does the mass change over the exposure time?
b.answer: As can be seen in the subfigure, the mass change increases with the exposure time for HF, but decreases for TMA.

c.question: Which feature is illustrated in the subfigure?
c.answer: The mass changes of the samples, in this case for HF, are shown as a function of the HF exposure time (s).
